intro_inherited: Your uncle just died. The good news? He left you everything.
A fat stack of cash, a garage with a questionable roof, and a car that... well, it's got four wheels. Mostly.
Time to hit the streets and make a name for yourself.
robbed: You pull up to the strip with your windows down and your wallet out like an absolute tourist.
Three guys you've never seen before box you in. It's over before your engine stops ticking.
They took everything. Every last credit. Even your good sunglasses.
You limp home on fumes with nothing but your dignity. Actually, they took that too.
junkyard: On the walk of shame home, you pass a junkyard. The fence has a hole. You have nothing to lose.
After an hour of tetanus-risk digging, you drag out some parts that almost look functional.
It's not much, but it's yours. Well, it's the junkyard's. But they won't miss it.
teach_inventory: You've got parts now. Questionable parts, but parts.
Use `/inventory` to see what you dragged out of that dumpster. You can tap any part in the list to inspect it.
teach_inspect: Not bad. Not good either, but not bad.
Want a closer look? Use `/inspect {card_name}` to check the stats. Each copy rolls slightly different — some are better than others.
teach_equip: Knowledge is power. Unfortunately, power is also power, and yours is currently sitting on the floor.
Use `/equip` to bolt these parts onto your ride. You need all **7 slots** filled before you can mint your rig.
Slots to fill: **Engine, Transmission, Tires, Chassis, Suspension, Brakes, Turbo**.
Or skip the grease work — `/autoequip best` will install your best parts automatically.
teach_build_preview: All 7 slots bolted. Your rig is starting to look like something.
Before you make it official, run `/build preview` — it shows which class your build is trending toward.
Class determines what races you're eligible for. It's not a choice — it comes from your stats.
teach_build_mint: See that class breakdown? That's your parts telling you what kind of car this is.
When you're ready to lock it in, use `/build mint` to issue your **Car Title**.
The title seals your **Engine** and **Chassis** — those are the core of the build. Swap tires, brakes, suspension, and turbo freely. Touch the core and you void the title.
teach_garage: Your rig has a name now. And a serial number. That's more than most things in this junkyard.
Use `/garage` to see your full build, class, and title details.
Want to scout the competition? `/peek @player` lets you see anyone's build.
teach_race: She runs. She stops. She turns. That's three whole features.
Remember that strip where you got jumped? Time to go back.
Use `/race @player` to send a challenge. They'll have to accept before you line up.
Most races are gated by **class** — Street is open to everyone, but Drag, Circuit, Drift, and Rally events require the right build. Your class is set by your stats, not your choice.
Some events go further and lock to a specific **body type** too — Drift Compact only, Rally Muscle only, that kind of thing. You'll know when you try to enter and can't.
Feeling bold? Add a wager — `/race @player wager:50` puts Creds on the line.
race_win: HOLY SHIT. You actually won. In THAT thing.
Your opponent is staring at their steering wheel trying to figure out what just happened.
Word on the street travels fast. Someone tosses you a pack as a "welcome to the scene" gift.
race_lose: You lost. Obviously. Look at your car.
But hey, you finished. That's more than your car's warranty would have predicted.
Some old guy in the crowd saw your effort and tosses you a pack. "Everyone starts somewhere, kid."
teach_pack: A free **Junkyard Pack**. Inside could be anything. Probably junk, but maybe slightly better junk.
It's already been opened for you. Let's see what you got...
teach_daily: One more thing. Every day you can use `/daily` to collect **100 Creds** and a **full set of parts** — one for every slot.
The rarity is random. Mostly junk, but every once in a while you pull something nasty.
outro: That's the basics. Build your ride, mint your title, race the streets, trade cards, and try not to wreck.
Upgrade your rig — swap wear parts (tires, brakes, suspension, turbo) any time. Replace your engine or chassis and you'll need to `/build disassemble` and re-mint.
The `/market` has parts for sale. `/trade` if you want to deal with other players directly.
Use `/multirace` to host a bigger event. Parts degrade each race, so keep an eye on your build.
Good luck out there. You're gonna need it.
